description: This figure displays the variation of logarithmic small-angle X-ray scattering (SAXS) intensity as a function of scattering vector q (unit: nm⁻¹) for four Fe-Al low-density steel samples with different Al contents: Fe-5Al, Fe-6Al, Fe-6.8Al, and Fe-9Al. Red open symbols (circle, square, triangle, diamond corresponding to the four alloys respectively) represent experimental SAXS data, while dark blue solid lines are fitted curves using Eq. (3) reported in the work. All curves are vertically offset to avoid overlapping. The SAXS intensity of all samples decreases with increasing q, and the low-q region matches the q⁻⁴ scaling relation noted in the caption. This SAXS data is used to quantitatively characterize the size, volume fraction and number density of nano-sized DO3 Fe3Al ordered precipitates in Fe-Al alloys, verifying the presence of uniformly dispersed ordered nanoprecipitates in all samples, and providing experimental evidence for subsequent strengthening mechanism analysis of these low-density steels.
